---
title: "Meta_Cima"
output: html_document
date: "2025-05-21"
---

```{r}
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(metafor)
library(CaMeA)
```

```{r}
merged <- fread("out2_ma_sample_2.csv")
merged <- merged[n.e > 0 & n.c > 0]
merged <- merged[!is.na(event.e) & !is.na(event.c)]
merged$group.no[is.na(merged$group.no)] <- 0
```

```{r}
# Single-group forest plot with study names
target_group <- list(comp.no = 1, outcome.no = 45, group.no = 0, cochrane_id = "CD012481")
measure <- "RR"

group_data <- merged[
  comp.no == target_group$comp.no & outcome.no == target_group$outcome.no &
  group.no == target_group$group.no & cochrane_id == target_group$cochrane_id
]

# Standard random-effects (carries study labels via slab)
dat <- escalc(measure = measure, ai = event.e, n1i = n.e,
              ci = event.c, n2i = n.c, data = group_data, slab = studlab)
res <- rma(yi, vi, data = dat, method = "DL")

# CaMeA aggregate estimate
agg <- camea(
  measure   = measure,
  ai        = group_data$event.e, n1i = group_data$n.e,
  ci        = group_data$event.c, n2i = group_data$n.c,
  log.scale = TRUE
)[[2]]

res_camea <- rma(yi = agg$estimate, sei = agg$se, method = "FE")

# Forest plot with both summaries
par(mar = c(2, 2, 2, 2))
forest(res, header = TRUE, top = 3,
       mlab = "Random-effects model",
       ylim = c(-3, nrow(dat) + 3))
addpoly(res_camea, row = -2, mlab = "Causal meta-analysis (CaMeA)")
```

```{r}
# Full loop: scatter plot comparing CaMeA vs random-effects
measures <- c("RD")

plot_data <- lapply(measures, function(measure) {
  use_log       <- measure %in% c("RR", "OR", "SR")
  unique_groups <- unique(merged[, .(comp.no, outcome.no, group.no, cochrane_id)])

  lapply(1:nrow(unique_groups), function(i) {
    row        <- unique_groups[i, ]
    group_data <- merged[
      comp.no == row$comp.no & outcome.no == row$outcome.no &
      group.no == row$group.no & cochrane_id == row$cochrane_id
    ]

    # Standard random-effects (metafor)
    res <- tryCatch({
      dat <- escalc(measure = measure, ai = event.e, n1i = n.e,
                    ci = event.c, n2i = n.c, data = group_data, slab = studlab,
                    add = 0.5, to = "only0")  # adds 0.5 only to studies with a zero cell
      rma(yi, vi, data = dat, method = "DL")
    }, error = function(e) NULL)

    # CaMeA
    agg <- tryCatch({
      camea(measure = measure,
            ai = group_data$event.e, n1i = group_data$n.e,
            ci = group_data$event.c, n2i = group_data$n.c,
            log.scale = use_log)[[2]]
    }, error = function(e) NULL)

    tibble(
      measure     = measure,
      comp.no     = row$comp.no,
      outcome.no  = row$outcome.no,
      group.no    = row$group.no,
      cochrane_id = row$cochrane_id,

      res_estimate  = if (!is.null(res)) as.numeric(res$b)     else NA_real_,
      res_ci_lb     = if (!is.null(res)) as.numeric(res$ci.lb) else NA_real_,
      res_ci_ub     = if (!is.null(res)) as.numeric(res$ci.ub) else NA_real_,

      res2_estimate = if (!is.null(agg)) agg$estimate                  else NA_real_,
      res2_ci_lb    = if (!is.null(agg)) agg$estimate - 1.96 * agg$se  else NA_real_,
      res2_ci_ub    = if (!is.null(agg)) agg$estimate + 1.96 * agg$se  else NA_real_
    )
  }) |> dplyr::bind_rows()
}) |> dplyr::bind_rows()

# Clean + discrepancy
clean_plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    !is.na(res_estimate), !is.na(res2_estimate),
    !is.na(res_ci_lb),    !is.na(res_ci_ub),
    !is.na(res2_ci_lb),   !is.na(res2_ci_ub)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(discrepancy = abs(res2_estimate - res_estimate))

# Highlight split
highlight_data <- clean_plot_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(comp.no == 1, outcome.no == 45, group.no == 0, cochrane_id == "CD012481")

normal_data <- clean_plot_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(!(comp.no == 1 & outcome.no == 45 & group.no == 0 & cochrane_id == "CD012481"))

# Scatter plot

p <- ggplot() +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(data = normal_data,
             aes(x = res_estimate, y = res2_estimate),
             size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8, colour = "#56B1F7") +
  geom_point(data = highlight_data,
             aes(x = res_estimate, y = res2_estimate),
             shape = 17, size = 4, colour = "black") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  labs(x = "Random-effects model", y = "Causal meta-analysis",
       title = paste(ifelse(measures %in% c("RR", "OR", "SR"), paste0("log(", measures, ")"), measures), collapse = ", ")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title   = element_text(size = 35, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 28),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 28)) +
  coord_fixed()

ggsave("RD.pdf", plot = p, width = 7, height = 7)
```
